agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to replace 1965 zoning bylaw with 2026 version
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on repealing Burnaby's old Zoning Bylaw from 1965 and replacing it with a new Zoning Bylaw 2026. This proposed change would also include new Development Permit Area Guidelines and reassign some properties to different zoning districts. If approved, the new bylaw would update the rules for what can be built and where, affecting many property owners and businesses in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on how the new zoning rules could affect your property or business.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
BURNABY ZONING BYLAW - PROPOSED REPEAL AND REPLACEMENT
Purpose: To seek Council approval to repeal Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignment.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Housing Authority Year 2 operating funding request
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation's request for Year 2 operating funding. This is a proposal to provide money to help the Housing Authority run its programs for another year. The decision has not been made yet — council will vote on whether to approve the funding at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2
BHA - ASSISTANCE REQUEST FOR YEAR 2 OPERATING FUNDING
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s assistance request for Year 2 Operating Funding.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2026 property tax rates for municipal, regional, and BIA
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on the proposed 2026 property tax rates for Burnaby. This includes the municipal portion (city services), the regional portion (for Metro Vancouver services), and Business Improvement Area (BIA) rates (extra taxes for businesses in specific areas that fund local improvements like marketing or street cleaning). If approved, these rates will determine how much property tax you pay in 2026. The decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this will directly affect your 2026 property tax bill.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3
2026 PROPERTY TAX RATES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for 2026 municipal, regional and business improvement area (BIA) property tax rates.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to appoint an Approving Officer
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to appoint an Approving Officer. An Approving Officer is a person who has the legal authority to approve plans for new subdivisions (splitting land into smaller lots) and road closures. This is a routine administrative appointment, but it affects anyone who wants to subdivide land or close a road in Burnaby. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider and vote on the appointment at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from you right now. Watch for the council decision — this appointment affects future subdivision and road-closure approvals.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4
APPOINTMENT OF APPROVING OFFICER
Purpose: To seek Council approval to appoint an Approving Officer.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on retaining wall height variance at 8012 11th Ave
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a Development Variance Permit (DVP) for a property at 8012 11th Avenue. A DVP is a special permission that lets a property owner break certain rules in the zoning bylaw. In this case, the owner wants to build retaining walls that are taller than what the current rules allow. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider the request at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you own or develop property near 8012 11th Avenue, watch for the council decision — it could set a precedent for similar height variances in your area.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5
DVP #26-01 - 8012 11TH AVENUE - RETAINING WALLS HEIGHT VARIANCES
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Development Variance Permit (DVP) #26-01.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to award insurance contracts for property and liability programs
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving the renewal of contracts for the city's annual property and liability insurance programs. This is a routine business decision to ensure the city has proper insurance coverage for its buildings, vehicles, and operations. The decision does not directly change any rules or fees for small businesses, but it affects how the city manages its own risk and financial obligations.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.6
2026 INSURANCE PROGRAMS - CONTRACT RENEWALS
Purpose: To request approval to award insurance contracts for the annual property and liability insurance programs.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to approve Heritage Commission members attending 2026 BC conference
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to allow members of Burnaby's Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC, from May 4-6, 2026. The conference is a professional development event for people involved in heritage conservation. If approved, the city would cover the costs for commission members to attend. This is not yet approved — council will decide at an upcoming meeting.

key_dates.label: Heritage BC Conference in Squamish — proposed attendance dates
Heritage BC Conference in Squamish — proposed attendance dates

agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.7
2026 HERITAGE BC CONFERENCE
Purpose: To seek Council approval for members of the Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC from May 4-6, 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract award for networking software licenses
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses. If approved, this would authorize the city to purchase the software licenses needed to keep its computer networks running. A 'contract award' is the formal decision to pick a company and agree to buy from them. This decision is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting and has not been finalized yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.8
CA - NETWORKING SOFTWARE LICENSES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for new civic facility furniture
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply, delivery, and installation of office and facility furniture for a new civic facility. If approved, this contract would cover all the furniture and equipment needed to fit out the new building. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.9
CA - NEW CIVIC FACILITY FIT-OUT, FURNISHINGS AND EQUIPMENT
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply, delivery and installation of office and facility furniture.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract change for external audit services
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change for the city's external audit services. This means the city wants to modify its existing agreement with the company that checks the city's financial records. If approved, this change would alter the terms of the audit contract. The decision is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.10
CC - EXTERNAL AUDIT SERVICES
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for External Audit Services.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for geotechnical work at Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change (a formal amendment to an existing agreement) for geotechnical consulting services at the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex. Geotechnical consultants study soil and ground conditions to ensure buildings are safe and stable. If approved, this change would adjust the scope or cost of the consultant's work on the project. The decision has not been made yet — council will discuss and vote on it at the upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from you at this time — this is an internal city contract decision. Watch for the council vote result if your business works on this project.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.11
CC - BURNABY LAKE RECREATION COMPLEX GEOTECHNICAL CONSULTING SERVICES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for geotechnical consultant services for the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex project.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on Spring 2026 community grant recipients
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving the recommended recipients for the Spring 2026 community grant intake. This decision will determine which local organizations and projects receive funding from the city. The grants are part of the city's regular program to support community groups, non-profits, and events that benefit Burnaby residents. If approved, the selected applicants will receive their grant money to carry out their proposed activities. The council meeting agenda lists this item for discussion and a final vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1
ECC - SPRING 2026 COMMUNITY GRANT APPLICATIONS
Purpose: To seek Council approval for the recommended grant recipients for the Spring 2026 intake.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Mid-year 2026 fee changes to Burnaby Fees and Charges Bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider mid-year 2026 amendments to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This proposed bylaw would update certain fees for various city services, permits, and licences. The changes are not yet approved — council will vote on them at an upcoming meeting. If passed, the new fees would take effect later in 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, these fee changes could affect your business licence or permit costs.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW - 2026 MID-YEAR AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To propose mid-year 2026 fee amendments to certain fee schedules within the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed Tenant Protection Bylaw and framework. If approved, this new bylaw (a local law) would create new rules to protect renters in the city. The proposal includes things like limits on certain rent increases and rules around evictions. Council will consider endorsing the framework and the bylaw at an upcoming meeting. This is not yet law — it is a proposal that council must vote on.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and consider attending or submitting comments to have your say on the proposed bylaw.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.3
PDC - PROPOSED TENANT PROTECTION FRAMEWORK AND TENANT PROTECTION BYLAW AND ASSOCIATED BYLAW AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement of the proposed tenant protection framework and Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw and associated bylaw amendments.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on Southeast Bikeway Upgrade improvements
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on proposed improvements for the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project. If approved, this project would add new bike lanes, crossings, and safety upgrades along the route. The decision is not final yet — Council will discuss and vote on the plan at an upcoming meeting. Business owners near the bikeway should be aware that construction could affect traffic, parking, and customer access in the area.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, prepare for possible construction impacts on your business access and parking.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.4
TC - SOUTHEAST BIKEWAY UPGRADE PROJECT - PROPOSED IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the proposed improvements as part of the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update design standards for safer intersections
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to update the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards. The change would require new street designs to include 'protected intersection geometry' — a safer layout that separates pedestrians and cyclists from turning vehicles. If approved, this would apply to new developments and road projects in the Town Centre area. The goal is to make walking and biking safer for people of all ages and abilities.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your development or construction project in Burnaby's Town Centre.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.5
TC - PROPOSED AMENDMENT TO THE BURNABY TOWN CENTRE DESIGN STANDARDS TO INCORPORATE PROTECTED INTERSECTION GEOMETRY
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement to amend the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards to incorporate protected intersection geometry, improve road safety for pedestrians and cyclists and support all-ages-and-abilities mobility.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby staff recommend against exempting Brentwood Park from SSMUH rules
agenda_briefs.summary: A staff report recommends Council not exempt Brentwood Park properties with restrictive covenants from provincial Small-Scale Multi-Unit Housing (SSMUH) requirements. The City is bound by provincial law to zone for SSMUH, and restrictive covenants are private interests the City is not party to. Amending the Building Bylaw to require covenant compliance would apply city-wide to all property types, increasing application costs and processing times, and is not recommended.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for Council's vote on the staff recommendation at the April 28, 2026 meeting.

key_dates.label: Council vote on Brentwood Park SSMUH exemption staff report

agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.1
BRENTWOOD PARK SSMUH MOTION - RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS AND STATUTORY BUILDING SCHEMES
Purpose: To respond to Council’s September 9, 2025, motion regarding exempting Brentwood Park properties with Restrictive Covenants from Provincial SSMUH requirements.

attachments.title: Brentwood Park SSMUH Motion - Restrictive Covenants and Statutory Building Schem

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive building permit activity report for March 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a report on building permits issued in Burnaby from March 1 to March 31, 2026. This report is for information only — it shows how much construction activity happened in the city during that month. No decision or vote is required on this item.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.2
SU: BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 03 FROM MARCH 1 - MARCH 31, 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction activity as reflected by the building permits that have been issued for the subject period.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Heritage designation for 3789 Royal Oak Avenue in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider an 'as-found report' and 'Statement of Significance' for the property at 3789 Royal Oak Avenue. A Statement of Significance is a formal document that explains why a building or site is historically important. If Council later votes to approve a heritage designation bylaw, this property could be legally protected as a heritage resource. That would mean any future changes to the building would need special permission. This item is on the agenda for information only — no final decision has been made yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.3
CHC - AS-FOUND REPORT AND STATEMENT OF SIGNIFICANCE FOR HERITAGE RESOURCES AT 3789 ROYAL OAK AVENUE
Purpose: To provide Council an as-found report and Statement of Significance for heritage resources located at 7389 Royal Oak Avenue for information.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby solid waste and recycling 2025 performance report
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a summary of the City of Burnaby's solid waste and recycling collection system performance for 2025. This report covers how well the city's garbage, recycling, and organics pickup services are working. A proposed bylaw or policy change could follow based on this report, but no decision has been made yet. Business owners who generate waste or recycling should watch for any upcoming changes to collection rules or fees.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on this report — it could lead to changes in waste collection rules or fees that affect your business.
agenda_briefs.industries: Food Service, Retail, Grocery & Food Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.4
EC - SOLID WASTE AND RECYCLING (SWR) 2025 ANNUAL REPORT
Purpose: To provide Council with a summary of the City of Burnaby solid waste and recycling collection system performance.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider update on food sustainability measures in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will receive an operational update on food sustainability measures. This is a report on current programs and activities, not a vote on a new bylaw or rule. The update will cover what the city is doing to support local food production, reduce food waste, and improve access to healthy food. No new rules or fees are proposed at this time. Council is set to hear the update and may ask staff questions or give direction for future work.
agenda_briefs.industries: Food Service, Grocery & Food Retail, Agriculture
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.5
EC - SU: ENHANCING FOOD SUSTAINABILITY IN BURNABY
Purpose: To provide Council with an operational update on food sustainability measures within the City of Burnaby.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Safety improvements at Central Boulevard and Metropolis parkade intersection
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and the Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access. The proposal aims to make the area safer for drivers, pedestrians, and cyclists. A vote on whether to move forward with these changes is expected at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.6
TC - CENTRAL BOULEVARD AND PARKADE INERSECTION SAFETY IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To provide Council with an overview of the proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezone 3900 Grand Promenade for hotel, restaurant, and 362 more homes
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw amendment to rezone 3900 Grand Promenade and nearby addresses. The developer wants to swap some planned office space for a hotel and restaurant, and add 362 more market strata units (privately owned homes in a multi-unit building). This would make Tower 1B seven storeys taller and Tower 1A four storeys taller than previously approved. The developer also proposes to move some required affordable housing commitments to a different site (the Carrigan site). This is a proposed change — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit written comments to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14808 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 7, 2026 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 6950 Austin Rd., 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place)
Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two market strata residential buildings of 80 (Tower 1B) and 73 (Tower 1A) storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. As part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to replace a portion of the commercial office use with hotel and restaurant uses, and add 362 additional market strata units from the development concept approved under REZ #18-32, resulting in an additional seven storeys for Tower 1B and four storeys for Tower 1A. In addition, as part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to provide the inclusionary requirement associated with Phase 1 only, to be transferred to the Carrigan site, which is an amendment from the previous rezoning approval.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 12-storey mixed-use development at 6535 Kingsway
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone 6535 Kingsway. The plan would allow a 12-storey building with 290 residential units, ground-floor shops facing Kingsway, and underground parking. This proposal matches the city's Official Community Plan and the Edmonds Community Plan. The decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit written comments to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14809 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 8, 2026 - REZ #25-09 (6535 Kingsway)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a 12-storey mixed-use multiple-family development with a double-height commercial storey at street level comprised of 290 residential units, ground-oriented commercial uses fronting Kingsway, and underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the applicable OCP and the Edmonds Community Plan designations for the site.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Five-storey self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw amendment to rezone 2757 Norland Avenue. If approved, the change would allow construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage building. The proposal follows the 1998 Official Community Plan and approved transition rules. This is not yet a done deal — the decision is up for a vote at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date and attend to voice your support or concerns.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14810 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 9, 2026 - REZ #25-21 (2757 Norland Avenue)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage building. The proposal is being advanced in accordance with the applicable 1998 OCP policies and approved transitionary provisions.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot bylaw amendment
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to Burnaby's Street and Traffic Bylaw that would create a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, this would change the rules for where and how these devices can be used on city streets. The decision has not been made yet — this is an upcoming proposal on the council agenda.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this pilot could affect how your business operates if you use or sell e-bikes or e-scooters.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot in parks
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would allow e-bikes and electric kick scooters in certain Burnaby parks as part of a pilot program. The amendment to the Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 would create new rules for where and how these devices can be used. This is not yet law — council will decide at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this pilot could affect where you can ride e-bikes and e-scooters in Burnaby parks.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment for e-bike and e-scooter pilot enforcement
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update how the city enforces rules for e-bikes and electric kick scooters as part of a pilot program. The amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 would allow the city to issue tickets and fines for things like improper parking, riding on sidewalks, or not following traffic rules. This is not yet law — it is up for approval at a future council meeting. If passed, the new rules would apply to anyone using an e-bike or e-scooter in Burnaby during the pilot program.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, e-bike and e-scooter riders must follow new enforcement rules.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026 (e-bike and electric kick scooter programs)
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2026 property tax rates bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would set the 2026 tax rates for all land and buildings (called 'improvements') in Burnaby. This bylaw is required under sections 197 and 216 of BC's Community Charter, which gives municipalities the power to set property tax rates each year. If approved, this bylaw will determine how much property tax you pay in 2026 based on the assessed value of your land and buildings.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this bylaw will directly affect your 2026 property tax bill.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter
